How I Lead

Leadership Philosophy

01
Data Before Opinion

Every hiring decision, every process change, every recommendation to leadership is grounded in evidence. In a function where gut feeling is the default, I bring numbers. Not to impress but to inform. The best talent strategies are built on what the data actually says, not what we assume it says.

02
Systems Over Individuals

I build infrastructure that outlasts any one person including me. Dashboards that update in real time. Trackers that capture every stage. Frameworks that make the next hire as well considered as the last. When I leave a function, the function should be stronger than when I arrived, regardless of who is in the role.

03
Dignity in Accountability

Performance must be visible but it must never be weaponised. I design productivity systems that show workload distribution and output clearly, so the right conversations can happen about resourcing, support, and growth. The goal is to lift the whole team, not to expose the weakest link.

Professional Experience

Senior Lead, Talent Acquisition
Team of 3 · 10 to 20 Hires Monthly · Executive Partnership

  • Developed a division based cost per hire model providing leadership with first ever visibility into recruitment cost efficiency across Sales, Operational, and Senior/Strategic functions.
  • Designed and deployed a rejection benchmarking framework capturing salary data on declined offers, enabling evidence based offer calibration by division.
  • Used advanced Boolean search techniques to source passive candidates for specialist and senior roles across multiple sectors.

Deep Dives

Case Studies

01
Analytics Infrastructure
Building a TA Intelligence Function from Zero
Context
High volume, multi division financial services company. No ATS, no analytics infrastructure, no cost visibility. Leadership making workforce decisions on instinct.
Problem
Zero real time visibility into pipeline health, stage conversion, time to hire, or recruitment cost. No ability to identify bottlenecks or justify headcount decisions with data.
Process
Built a 39 column Google Sheets tracker, designed a live HTML dashboard with dual data upload, added a cost per hire engine, created a rejection benchmarking framework, and produced quarterly executive reports.
Result
Full division level analytics operational. GM bottleneck identified and tracked. Offer acceptance benchmarked by division and salary band. Cost per hire modelled from ₦95k to ₦286k by role type.
02
Process Transformation
Cutting the Hiring Cycle by 48% in a Health Tech Scale Up
Context
Fast scaling health tech company with aggressive growth targets and revenue critical roles going unfilled for weeks.
Problem
21 day average hiring cycle creating direct commercial risk. No standardised job briefs, inconsistent screening, and interview scheduling delays adding unnecessary days at every stage.
Process
Audited every stage for delay sources. Standardised job briefs, built screening scorecards, created interview workflow templates, and introduced SLA based scheduling with hiring manager accountability.
Result
21 days to 11 days. 48% cycle reduction. 60% improvement in candidate quality. Revenue critical roles filled faster without compromising selection rigour.
03
Function Design
Building Scalable Hiring Frameworks for Growth Stage Founders
Context
Multiple Nigerian startups across engineering, fintech, product, and operations hiring reactively with no process, no scorecards, and high mis hire rates.
Problem
Founders hiring based on personal networks and gut feeling. No job architecture, no pipeline discipline. Mis hires costing 3 to 6 months of wasted salary and lost momentum.
Process
Designed job architectures from scratch, conducted talent audits and workforce diagnostics, built repeatable hiring frameworks tailored to each company's stage, and implemented channel segmentation for faster sourcing.
Result
250+ roles filled across 5 years. 40% sourcing turnaround improvement. Multiple founders reporting their first structured, auditable hiring process.
Original Methodologies

Strategic Frameworks

Division Based Cost per Hire Model

A three tier cost model covering Sales, Operational, and Senior/Strategic divisions that calculates internal cost from stakeholder time and hourly rate, and external cost from job boards, assessments, and agency fees per role type.

GM Bottleneck Tracking Methodology

A stage date tracking system that isolates two key delays in non sales hiring: TA pack preparation time and GM approval wait time, enabling evidence based SLA conversations with leadership.

Offer Rejection Benchmarking Framework

A candidate level rejection log capturing actual offer amounts, primary and secondary rejection reasons, and division — producing average rejected offer thresholds and suggested minimum offer figures per division.

Stage Drop Off and Funnel Conversion Model

A pipeline analytics model tracking candidates at every recruitment stage, computing drop off rates between each stage and identifying where the biggest volume losses occur in the funnel for each division.

Competency Based Assessment Architecture

A structured evaluation framework combining psychometric assessment, technical testing, and competency based interview scorecards to standardise candidate evaluation across role types and reduce subjective decision making.

TA Capacity Utilisation Model

A workload framework mapping individual TA role ownership to stage headcounts and expected hours per stage, enabling visibility into team capacity and workload distribution across divisions.
